title: Accessible evacuation assistance agreement
summary: Ask the person what help, communication method, mobility device, medication, sensory support, and privacy they prefer before an emergency.
content: Ask the person what help, communication method, mobility device, medication, sensory support, and privacy they prefer before an emergency. Assign primary and backup helpers who have practised the route with consent. Do not separate a person from essential mobility, communication, service-animal, or medical equipment unless immediate life safety requires it.
materials: Notebook or printed card. Context: “Accessible evacuation assistance agreement”.
Relevant labels, contacts, and official alerts — for «Accessible evacuation assistance agreement»
steps: Ask the person what help, communication method, mobility device, medication, sensory support, and privacy they prefer before an emergency.
Assign primary and backup helpers who have practised the route with consent.
Assign one person to act and another to verify the result for Accessible evacuation assistance agreement.
Record time, source, action, result, unresolved risk, and next review for Accessible evacuation assistance agreement.
warnings: Do not separate a person from essential mobility, communication, service-animal, or medical equipment unless immediate life safety requires it.
Official instructions and trained responders override this offline card for Accessible evacuation assistance agreement.
common_mistakes: Acting before location, time, hazard, and responsible person are clear for Accessible evacuation assistance agreement.
Failing to update the plan when conditions change during Accessible evacuation assistance agreement.
alternatives: Use a paper note, SMS, radio message, or trusted messenger when the main system is unavailable for Accessible evacuation assistance agreement.
failure_signs: The decision source, responsible person, or next review cannot be identified for Accessible evacuation assistance agreement.
The action creates a new hazard or delays qualified help during Accessible evacuation assistance agreement.
when_not_to_use: Do not use this card to override an evacuation order, emergency responder, clinician, product label, or local law for Accessible evacuation assistance agreement.
short_term: For Accessible evacuation assistance agreement, stabilize the immediate risk, communicate the decision, and schedule the next check before attention moves elsewhere.
long_term: After Accessible evacuation assistance agreement, update supplies, contact lists, maps, training, and the written plan from what actually happened.
if_method_fails: Stop Accessible evacuation assistance agreement, isolate the unsafe result, communicate the failure, and use the safest official or professionally supported fallback available.
environment_notes: Adapt Accessible evacuation assistance agreement to Greek heat, wildfire smoke, earthquakes, islands, mountain roads, apartment stairs, power cuts, water interruptions, mobility limits, and official alerts.
sources: CDC — Water, sanitation and hygiene emergencies: https://www.cdc.gov/water-emergency/index.html
CDC — Keep food safe after an emergency: https://www.cdc.gov/food-safety/foods/keep-food-safe-after-emergency.html
American Red Cross — First aid steps: https://www.redcross.org/take-a-class/first-aid/performing-first-aid/first-aid-steps
